content.text:
<user_query>
how to find the credit usage on terms of usd 
</user_query>

Checking Cursor billing docs and the repo for how credit usage is shown in USD.

[REDACTED]

input.search_term: Cursor IDE credit usage USD billing how to check 2025 2026
input.explanation: Find how Cursor displays credit usage in USD for the user.

input.prompt: How does a Cursor user find their credit usage in USD? Where in the dashboard or IDE is usage shown in dollar amounts? Include Usage tab, Billing & Invoices, in-app credit counter, and any relevant settings paths.

input.search_term: cursor.com/dashboard usage tab USD cost per request
input.explanation: Find specific UI locations where USD amounts are shown for Cursor usage.

content.text:
If you mean **Cursor credit usage in USD**, here’s where to look and what to expect.

## 1. Web dashboard (main place)

Go to **[cursor.com/dashboard](https://cursor.com/dashboard)** while signed in.

### Usage tab
Open **[Usage](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=usage)** for the detailed breakdown:

- **Total usage** — often shown as a **cumulative USD total** at the top (how much of your monthly budget you’ve used).
- **Per request** — the **Cost** column shows dollar amounts **after** you pass your included quota. While you’re still within plan limits, many rows show **“Included”** instead of a dollar value.
- **By model / date** — you can see which models consumed the most.

### Billing & Invoices
Open **Billing & Invoices** in the dashboard:

- **Included Usage** — usage covered by your subscription (in USD).
- **On-Demand Usage** — anything charged above your plan (in USD).

This is the clearest place for **actual charges**, not just percentages.

## 2. In the Cursor app

You can also monitor usage inside the editor:

1. Open **Cursor Settings** (gear icon or `Ctrl + ,`)
2. Go to **Dashboard** (or account/usage-related settings)
3. Enable **Show Credit Counter** and **Display Reset Timer**

That gives you a live view of remaining credits and when they reset.

## 3. What your plan includes (USD budget)

From Cursor’s docs, monthly **included API usage** is roughly:

| Plan   | Monthly included API usage |
|--------|----------------------------|
| Pro    | $20                        |
| Pro+   | $70                        |
| Ultra  | $400                       |

**Auto** and **Composer** are tracked separately and don’t draw from that API pool the same way.

## 4. If you don’t see USD per request

Cursor has been changing the Usage UI. While you’re within included usage, the dashboard may show:

- **Percentages** instead of dollar amounts, or
- **“Included”** in the Cost column instead of `$0.xx`

Workarounds:

- Check **Billing & Invoices** for USD totals.
- Use **Export CSV** on the Usage page — some users report cost data is still in the export even when the web UI hides it.
- After you exceed included usage (with on-demand enabled), the **Cost** column usually starts showing real **USD** amounts.

## 5. Reset date

Credits reset on your **billing date** (the day you subscribed), not the 1st of the month. Find it under **Manage Subscription** in the dashboard.

---

**Quick path:** [cursor.com/dashboard?tab=usage](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=usage) for breakdown → **Billing & Invoices** for USD charges.

If you meant credit usage for something else (AWS, a different tool, or this coaching repo), say which product and I can point you to the right place.

[REDACTED]
